description_long: This bird features a slender, elongated body with a slightly curved, pointed beak and a long tail. Its plumage is predominantly grayish-brown with a pattern of dark spots and bars along the back and wings, and a subtle reddish patch near the lower face and neck. The wings and tail are marked with darker, more defined barring, while the underparts are lighter with scattered spots. The legs are short and appear gray, blending with the overall muted coloration. The eyes are small and dark, set above the distinctive beak. This bird is well-adapted for perching and foraging in urban parks, gardens, and wooded areas.
description: A slender, grayish-brown bird with a pointed beak, spotted and barred plumage, and a long tail. It has a reddish patch near the face and short gray legs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's elongated body, pointed beak, and the reddish patch near the lower face.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the symmetrical barring on the back and the long, straight tail feathers.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the spotted and barred plumage, with the beak and reddish face patch visible.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the pointed beak, small head, and the subtle gradient of color from the face to the underparts.
attribute_plumage_pattern: spotted and barred with dark spots on the underparts and barring on the wings and tail
